encryptionConfig.description: An EncryptionConfig object that contains the KMS key ARN that is used for encryption. By default, CodeGuru Security uses an AWS-managed key for encryption. To specify your own key, call UpdateAccountConfiguration. If you do not specify a customer-managed key, returns empty.

kmsKeyArn.description: The KMS key ARN that is used for encryption. If an AWS-managed key is used for encryption, returns empty.

summaries.description: A list of ScanSummary objects with information about all scans in an account.

nextToken.description: A pagination token. You can use this in future calls to ListScans to continue listing results after the current page.

scanState.description: The state of the scan. A scan can be In Progress, Complete, or Failed.

createdAt.description: The time when the scan was created.

updatedAt.description: The time the scan was last updated. A scan is updated when it is re-run.

findings.description: A list of all findings which were successfully fetched.

failedFindings.description: A list of errors for individual findings which were not fetched. Each BatchGetFindingsError contains the scanName, findingId, errorCode and error message.

createdAt.description: The time when the finding was created.

generatorId.description: The identifier for the component that generated a finding such as AmazonCodeGuruSecurity.

updatedAt.description: The time when the finding was last updated. Findings are updated when you remediate them or when the finding code location changes.

status.description: The status of the finding. A finding status can be open or closed.

resource.description: The resource where Amazon CodeGuru Security detected a finding.

id.description: The scanName of the scan that was run on the resource.

subResourceId.description: The identifier for a section of the resource.

vulnerability.description: An object that describes the detected security vulnerability.

referenceUrls.description: One or more URL addresses that contain details about a vulnerability.

relatedVulnerabilities.description: One or more vulnerabilities that are related to the vulnerability being described.

filePath.description: An object that describes the location of the detected security vulnerability in your code.

path.description: The path to the resource with the security vulnerability.

startLine.description: The first line number of the code snippet where the security vulnerability appears in your code.

endLine.description: The last line number of the code snippet where the security vulnerability appears in your code.

codeSnippet.description: A list of CodeLine objects that describe where the security vulnerability appears in your code.

itemCount.description: The number of times the vulnerability appears in your code.

severity.description: The severity of the finding. Severity can be critical, high, medium, low, or informational. For information on severity levels, see Finding severity in the Amazon CodeGuru Security User Guide.

remediation.description: An object that contains the details about how to remediate a finding.

recommendation.description: An object that contains information about the recommended course of action to remediate a finding.

text.description: The recommended course of action to remediate the finding.

url.description: The URL address to the recommendation for remediating the finding.

suggestedFixes.description: A list of SuggestedFix objects. Each object contains information about a suggested code fix to remediate the finding.

detectorTags.description: One or more tags or categorizations that are associated with a detector. These tags are defined by type, programming language, or other classification such as maintainability or consistency.

detectorId.description: The identifier for the detector that detected the finding in your code. A detector is a defined rule based on industry standards and AWS best practices.

detectorName.description: The name of the detector that identified the security vulnerability in your code.

ruleId.description: The identifier for the rule that generated the finding.

runId.description: UUID that identifies the individual scan run.

resourceId.description: The identifier for the resource object that contains resources that were scanned.

scanState.description: The current state of the scan. Returns either InProgress, Successful, or Failed.

codeArtifactId.description: The identifier for the code file uploaded to the resource object. Returned by CreateUploadUrl when you upload resources to be scanned.

s3Url.description: A pre-signed S3 URL. You can upload the code file you want to scan with the required requestHeaders using any HTTP client.

requestHeaders.description: A set of key-value pairs that contain the required headers when uploading your resource.

codeArtifactId.description: The identifier for the uploaded code resource. Pass this to CreateScan to use the uploaded resources.

findings.description: A list of findings generated by the specified scan.

nextToken.description: A pagination token. You can use this in future calls to GetFindings to continue listing results after the current page.

createdAt.description: The time when the finding was created.

generatorId.description: The identifier for the component that generated a finding such as AmazonCodeGuruSecurity.

updatedAt.description: The time when the finding was last updated. Findings are updated when you remediate them or when the finding code location changes.

status.description: The status of the finding. A finding status can be open or closed.

resource.description: The resource where Amazon CodeGuru Security detected a finding.

id.description: The scanName of the scan that was run on the resource.

subResourceId.description: The identifier for a section of the resource.

vulnerability.description: An object that describes the detected security vulnerability.

referenceUrls.description: One or more URL addresses that contain details about a vulnerability.

relatedVulnerabilities.description: One or more vulnerabilities that are related to the vulnerability being described.

filePath.description: An object that describes the location of the detected security vulnerability in your code.

path.description: The path to the resource with the security vulnerability.

startLine.description: The first line number of the code snippet where the security vulnerability appears in your code.

endLine.description: The last line number of the code snippet where the security vulnerability appears in your code.

codeSnippet.description: A list of CodeLine objects that describe where the security vulnerability appears in your code.

itemCount.description: The number of times the vulnerability appears in your code.

severity.description: The severity of the finding. Severity can be critical, high, medium, low, or informational. For information on severity levels, see Finding severity in the Amazon CodeGuru Security User Guide.

remediation.description: An object that contains the details about how to remediate a finding.

recommendation.description: An object that contains information about the recommended course of action to remediate a finding.

text.description: The recommended course of action to remediate the finding.

url.description: The URL address to the recommendation for remediating the finding.

suggestedFixes.description: A list of SuggestedFix objects. Each object contains information about a suggested code fix to remediate the finding.

detectorTags.description: One or more tags or categorizations that are associated with a detector. These tags are defined by type, programming language, or other classification such as maintainability or consistency.

detectorId.description: The identifier for the detector that detected the finding in your code. A detector is a defined rule based on industry standards and AWS best practices.

detectorName.description: The name of the detector that identified the security vulnerability in your code.

ruleId.description: The identifier for the rule that generated the finding.

metricsSummary.description: The summary metrics from the specified date.

date.description: The date from which the metrics summary information was retrieved.

openFindings.description: The number of open findings of each severity.

info.description: A numeric value corresponding to an informational finding.

low.description: A numeric value corresponding to a low severity finding.

medium.description: A numeric value corresponding to a medium severity finding.

high.description: A numeric value corresponding to a high severity finding.

critical.description: A numeric value corresponding to a critical finding.

categoriesWithMostFindings.description: A list of CategoryWithFindingNum objects for the top 5 finding categories with the most findings.

scansWithMostOpenFindings.description: A list of ScanNameWithFindingNum objects for the top 3 scans with the most number of open findings.

scansWithMostOpenCriticalFindings.description: A list of ScanNameWithFindingNum objects for the top 3 scans with the most number of open critical findings.

runId.description: UUID that identifies the individual scan run.

scanState.description: The current state of the scan. Returns either InProgress, Successful, or Failed.

analysisType.description: The type of analysis CodeGuru Security performed in the scan, either Security or All. The Security type only generates findings related to security. The All type generates both security findings and quality findings.

updatedAt.description: The time when the scan was last updated. Only available for STANDARD scan types.

numberOfRevisions.description: The number of times a scan has been re-run on a revised resource.

errorMessage.description: Details about the error that causes a scan to fail to be retrieved.

findingsMetrics.description: A list of AccountFindingsMetric objects retrieved from the specified time interval.

nextToken.description: A pagination token. You can use this in future calls to ListFindingMetrics to continue listing results after the current page.

date.description: The date from which the findings metrics were retrieved.

newFindings.description: The number of new findings of each severity on the specified date.

info.description: A numeric value corresponding to an informational finding.

low.description: A numeric value corresponding to a low severity finding.

medium.description: A numeric value corresponding to a medium severity finding.

high.description: A numeric value corresponding to a high severity finding.

critical.description: A numeric value corresponding to a critical finding.

closedFindings.description: The number of closed findings of each severity on the specified date.

info.description: A numeric value corresponding to an informational finding.

low.description: A numeric value corresponding to a low severity finding.

medium.description: A numeric value corresponding to a medium severity finding.

high.description: A numeric value corresponding to a high severity finding.

critical.description: A numeric value corresponding to a critical finding.

openFindings.description: The number of open findings of each severity as of the specified date.

info.description: A numeric value corresponding to an informational finding.

low.description: A numeric value corresponding to a low severity finding.

medium.description: A numeric value corresponding to a medium severity finding.

high.description: A numeric value corresponding to a high severity finding.

critical.description: A numeric value corresponding to a critical finding.

meanTimeToClose.description: The average time in days it takes to close findings of each severity as of a specified date.

info.description: A numeric value corresponding to an informational finding.

low.description: A numeric value corresponding to a low severity finding.

medium.description: A numeric value corresponding to a medium severity finding.

high.description: A numeric value corresponding to a high severity finding.

critical.description: A numeric value corresponding to a critical finding.

tags.description:
An array of key-value pairs used to tag an existing scan. A tag is a custom attribute label with two parts:

  • A tag key. For example, CostCenter, Environment, or Secret. Tag keys are case sensitive.

  • An optional tag value field. For example, 111122223333, Production, or a team name. Omitting the tag value is the same as using an empty string. Tag values are case sensitive.

encryptionConfig.description: An EncryptionConfig object that contains the KMS key ARN that is used for encryption. If you did not specify a customer-managed KMS key in the request, returns empty.

kmsKeyArn.description: The KMS key ARN that is used for encryption. If an AWS-managed key is used for encryption, returns empty.
